# Catalog Conversion: i18next JSON → Lingui PO

## Overview

i18next uses JSON files with string keys. Lingui uses `.po` (gettext) files where the source message text is extracted directly from your code.

**i18next structure:**
```
locales/
  en/translation.json
  de/translation.json
```

**Lingui structure (generated by `lingui extract`):**
```
src/locales/
  en/messages.po   ← source locale, auto-populated
  de/messages.po   ← translation locale, translators fill msgstr
```

## Approach 1: Message-based IDs (Recommended)

Write Lingui macros with the message text directly in the source code. `lingui extract` generates all catalog files automatically - no manual catalog work needed.

```jsx
<Trans>Hello World</Trans>
t`Submit form`
```

```bash
npx lingui extract   # generates .po files from source code
npx lingui compile   # compiles .po → .js message catalogs
```

Non-source locale `.po` files will have empty `msgstr` fields. Send them to translators or import existing translations via your TMS.

## Approach 2: Preserve i18next Keys as Explicit IDs

Use this when you want to keep existing translation keys (e.g., to avoid re-translating in your TMS).

In source code, pass an explicit `id` matching the old key:
```jsx
<Trans id="nav.home">Home</Trans>
t({ id: "nav.home", message: "Home" })
```

Run `lingui extract` - it generates `.po` files with `msgid` set to your explicit IDs and empty `msgstr` fields. Populate translations via your TMS or manually in the `.po` files.

> **Note:** i18next plural keys (`key_one`/`key_other`) must be merged into a single ICU MessageFormat entry in the `.po` file:
> ```po
> msgid "{count, plural, one {# item} other {# items}}"
> msgstr "{count, plural, one {# Artikel} other {# Artikel}}"
> ```

## Namespace Handling

i18next namespaces map to separate Lingui catalogs defined in `lingui.config.js`:

```js
catalogs: [
  { path: "<rootDir>/src/locales/{locale}/common", include: ["src/components/common"] },
  { path: "<rootDir>/src/locales/{locale}/auth",   include: ["src/components/auth"] },
]
```

`lingui extract` generates a separate `.po` file per catalog automatically.

## PO File Format Reference

```po
#. Comment for translators (from `comment` prop)
#: src/App.tsx:42
msgctxt "direction"
msgid "right"
msgstr "rechts"
```

| Field | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `msgid` | Source string or explicit ID |
| `msgstr` | Translation |
| `msgctxt` | Context (from `context` prop) |
| `#:` | File and line reference |
| `#.` | Translator comment |

## Verifying the Migration

```bash
npx lingui extract   # re-extracts; shows Language | Total | Missing table
npx lingui compile   # compiles .po → .js
npx lingui compile --typescript   # for TypeScript projects
```

A successful migration shows `Missing: 0` for each locale.
